title: erigeron howellii
intro: Erigeron howellii, also known as Howell's fleabane, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to the Pacific coastal regions of North America, and its medicinal properties have been utilized by various cultures for centuries. The plant contains bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, providing health benefits including wound healing and reducing inflammation. Erigeron howellii can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, or salves, which are commonly used to treat various health issues, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy.
